cards.text: “We Will Rebuild” Enter a LEGO set number and get alternative build ideas using only pieces from that set.
“Doctor Beat” Upload or describe your song structure and get feedback on intro length, drops, transitions, variation, and listener momentum.
Upload a photo or artwork and convert it into a LEGO mosaic plan with part counts, colour mapping, and build instructions. Already exists - how would mine be better?

“Upload a loop or sample and get a visual/metadata mood board: genre tags, texture words, BPM estimate, key estimate, and suggested arrangement ideas. Not sure how useful this would be, might be supplemental features of a bigger thing.

“Neighborhood Soundwatch” An AI-assisted sample library browser that auto-tags loops by BPM, key, genre, instrument, mood, and energy. There’s already a number of these (Like XO by XLN), so it would need some stand-out features.
“Upload or enter a tracklist and get a suggested DJ set order based on BPM, key, energy, genre, and mood arc.
”PIAB (Party In A Box)” Generate themed crates for gigs, parties, radio shows, or mixes, such as “sunset warm-up house” or “late-night leftfield breaks.”
” A website that turns a DJ setlist into a visual energy journey, showing peaks, breathers, tempo shifts, and mood changes. Could be merged with PIAB.
A songwriter’s rhyme assistant that avoids cliché rhymes and suggests more interesting near-rhymes, slant rhymes, and internal rhymes.
“JIWW (Jump In, Water’s Wet)” Generate a complete production brief for a new track: tempo, key, drum style, synth palette, bass approach, arrangement, and reference vibe.
Acrilyc Pour idea exploration
Article: Why AI Coding Agents Need Product Managers More Than Ever As agents write more code, PM skill shifts toward framing, constraints, QA, and taste.
The Case for Algorithmic Creativity Argue that randomness, rules, MIDI patterns, and Canvas APIs can produce meaningful creative work without generative AI.
The Anti-SaaS Portfolio: Why Some Apps Should Just Be Weird A philosophy piece arguing that not every app needs a market, pricing page, or growth loop. Some should exist as artifacts.

How to Judge an AI-Built App When You Didn’t Hand-Code Every Line A useful PM/creator rubric: concept, interaction quality, maintainability, accessibility, performance, security, and emotional response.
Polish project page layouts
"Expecto" neural network sandbox. Visual way to understand how NNs work. Do some fancy things with animation.
Turning Weird Little Ideas Into Buildable Apps - My practical process for moving from vague concept to AI-readable PRD to deployable project.
"Splinched" Automated sample chopper. Test the limits of serverless.
"Sound Of Wall" - the opposite of a wall of sound. Take music and make art that can hang on a wall from it.
